Motivation: If the formal derivation graph later exhibits structural features resembling CNNA objects - layering, boundary cuts, operators on graphs, signal flow, modular clusters - this should be studied as a meta-level observation, not as a premise.

Raw data / tools: discipline subgraphs for Dirichlet/DtN/Schur, prenumerical/ToC/Substrate, Topology/Algebra/CD; module clusters; graph cuts; source-sink paths; proof-dossier links.

Derived-only boundary: Strictly comparison/diagnostic. Any apparent self-similarity between theory and formalization remains an observation unless separately formalized and proved.
Plan action: Add Phase 19.4 and object LEG19; keep outside the active implementation path.
